title: Middle East conflict intensifies with Israel and Iran exchanges
summary: The Israeli military began a wide‑scale new wave of strikes on Iranian targets, while Hezbollah continues rocket barrages toward northern Israel. At least 17 people were reported injured in airstrikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs as tensions escalate in the region’s most severe conflict phase.

title: Global oil prices spike amid Middle East disruptions
summary: Crude oil prices surged past $110 per barrel after attacks on tankers and infrastructure in Iraqi waters heightened fears of supply disruptions. Market volatility continues as impacts of the conflict ripple through global energy markets.

title: India hosts Global Conference on Women in Agri‑Food Systems
summary: India will host the Global Conference on Women in Agri‑Food Systems (GCWAS‑2026) from 12‑14 March in New Delhi. The event focuses on promoting inclusion and strengthening women’s roles in agriculture worldwide.

title: Asia tech sector expands AI and digital innovations
summary: Leading tech companies in Asia are advancing AI and digital innovation, particularly in healthcare and robotics, positioning the region as a major global technology hub.

title: Regional diplomacy efforts increase amid geopolitical tensions
summary: Nigeria and neighbouring countries engage in enhanced regional diplomacy talks focused on trade and security amid ongoing international tensions.

title: IPL 2026 schedule revealed as domestic cricket builds momentum
summary: The Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 schedule was released, with key fixtures set for late March as anticipation builds ahead of the tournament’s start.

title: Energy ministers pledge coordinated action to address crude price surge
summary: G7 energy ministers declared readiness to take coordinated measures to stabilise energy markets, responding to crude price spikes triggered by disruptions in the Middle East.

title: Education reforms expand health and medical seats in India
summary: The Indian government approved more than 11,000 MBBS seats and nearly 9,000 postgraduate medical seats for the 2025‑26 academic year, strengthening healthcare education capacity.
